behaviors.description: A plugin whose manifest is missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin is executed.
behaviors.justification: Integration is the minimum because the failure is at a module boundary: manifest validation must gate module import/execution. Unit-floor evidence: "manifest is missing a required field" + "rejected before any code from that plugin is executed" (validator/loader boundary). Ceiling-stop evidence: "loads plugin packages from a configured directory" (local in-process filesystem, no subprocess/network/external API) and no AI/ML behavior is described. Rejected higher levels: not System (no real HTTP/subprocess/OS-dependent primitive beyond ordinary local file reads), not Agentic/Workflow (no model judgment or agent pipeline).
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugin directory with one invalid plugin manifest and an entry module that writes a sentinel file at import time. Run the real loader over that directory. Assert the loader reports validation rejection and the sentinel side effect never occurs.

behaviors.description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B, plugin B's `onLoad` hook is called before plugin A's.
behaviors.justification: Integration is the minimum because this is resolver-to-executor orchestration across multiple plugins. Unit-floor evidence: "plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B" and "plugin B's `onLoad` hook is called before plugin A's" (dependency graph + hook runner boundary). Ceiling-stop evidence: plugins are loaded from a local "configured directory" and behavior concerns hook order only; no subprocess/network and no AI/ML decision. Rejected higher levels: not System (no external infra dependency), not Agentic/Workflow (no LLM steps).
behaviors.test_description: Use two real plugin folders in a temp directory. A manifest depends on B. Each `onLoad` appends its name to a shared in-memory/log sink. Run loader and assert observed order is `[B, A]`.

behaviors.description: When two plugins each declare a dependency on the other, the loader reports the cycle and loads neither.
behaviors.justification: Integration is the minimum because failure requires wiring cycle detection to load suppression/reporting across plugins. Unit-floor evidence: "two plugins each declare a dependency on the other" and "loader reports the cycle and loads neither" (graph analysis + loader control-flow boundary). Ceiling-stop evidence: scope is local plugin loading from directory/manifest data; no real network/subprocess/OS event dependency and no model-based judgment. Rejected higher levels: not System, not Agentic/Workflow for the same disqualifying text.
behaviors.test_description: Create two plugin manifests (A depends on B, B depends on A) with entry modules that would emit side effects if loaded. Run loader and assert cycle error is reported and neither plugin’s import/`onLoad` side effects occur.

behaviors.description: A plugin's `onLoad` hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: Integration is the minimum because correctness is the handoff between module import/execution and lifecycle dispatcher. Unit-floor evidence: "`onLoad` hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed" (importer/runtime boundary to hook runner). Ceiling-stop evidence: behavior is fully in-process Node module loading from local plugin packages; no subprocess/network/external API and no AI/ML output dependency. Rejected higher levels: not System, not Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Create a plugin whose entry module sets a global/state flag during import, and whose `onLoad` asserts that flag is already set. Run loader and assert `onLoad` succeeds only when import side effect happened first.

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws an error during its `onLoad` hook, the loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: Integration is the minimum because this is cross-component error isolation plus dependency-aware continuation. Unit-floor evidence: "plugin throws an error during its `onLoad` hook" and "loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it" (hook executor, dependency graph, and scheduler boundary). Ceiling-stop evidence: local plugin framework behavior only (directory/manifest/hooks), with no subprocess/network and no AI/ML decision path. Rejected higher levels: not System, not Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Set up plugins A, B, C where B depends on A and C is independent. Make A throw in `onLoad`. Run loader and assert: A failure recorded, B not initialized, C initialized successfully.
